window_summary: This window tracks the recent godotengine/godot release stream across major and minor versions, including feature releases like 3.5, 4.0, 4.1, 4.2, 4.3, 4.4, 4.5, 4.6, and 3.6, along with their maintenance updates. It covers the evolution from the 3.x branch through the 4.x series, highlighting new features, performance improvements, and bug fixes.

chunks.title: 3.5-stable
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: godotengine/godot
Release: 3.5-stable
Tag: 3.5-stable; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2022-08-05T10:00:09Z; created_at: 2022-08-05T01:40:16Z
Release notes:
**Godot 3.5** is a major new milestone with improvements all around, backported from the development branch of Godot 4.0, or written for the `3.x` branch's specific needs. It is absolutely feature-packed with new features such as:
New NavigationServer with obstacle avoidance; improved tweening with SceneTreeTween; Time singleton; Label3D and TextMesh nodes; unique node names for easy access; flow containers; 3D physics interpolation; asynchronous shader compilation and caching; OccluderShapePolygon; material overlays; Android editor port; improved VCS features; and more!
- [Release notes](https://godotengine.org/article/godot-3-5-cant-stop-wont-stop)
- [Curated changelog](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/3.5-stable/CHANGELOG.md)
- [Git changelog](https://downloads.tuxfamily.org/godotengine/3.5/Godot_v3.5-stable_changelog_authors.txt)
- [Download](https://downloads.tuxfamily.org/godotengine/3.5)
*All files for this release are mirrored under **Assets** below.*
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the godotengine/godot release 3.5-stable, Godot 3.5 is a major milestone featuring a new NavigationServer with obstacle avoidance, improved tweening with SceneTreeTween, a Time singleton, Label3D and TextMesh nodes, unique node names for easy access, flow containers, 3D physics interpolation, asynchronous shader compilation and caching, OccluderShapePolygon, material overlays, an Android editor port, and improved VCS features.

chunks.title: 4.0-stable
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: godotengine/godot
Release: 4.0-stable
Tag: 4.0-stable; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2023-03-01T13:02:22Z; created_at: 2023-03-01T00:38:07Z
Release notes:
4 years of development.
12,000 merged pull requests.
7,000 fixed issues.
1,500 individual contributors across engine and docs.
The Godot 4.0 release is by all metrics our biggest release so far.
No stone has been left unturned, all parts of the engine have been
modernized, refactored, overhauled, rewritten, redesigned.
Our work is far from done. Many areas still have significant known issues,
and will require focused work from all willing contributors to fix blocking
bugs, implement missing features, optimize for performance or compatibility,
and improve the user experience.
But Godot 4.0 marks the start of the new, modern Godot Engine, and a solid
foundation for us all to build upon. Future 4.x releases will come with a
much faster cadence, enabling us to iterate quickly on new features and
improvements to what we already provide.
To all of you who were involved in making Godot 4.0 what it is today, however
big or small your contributions were:
**THANK YOU!**
This was a massive undertaking, and you all participated in unique and
wonderful ways to build a free and open source game engine for everyone to
use and enjoy. You are breathtaking! <3
- [Release notes](https://godotengine.org/article/godot-4-0-sets-sail/)
- [Release highlights by GDQuest](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=chXAjMQrcZk)
- [Git changelog (by authors)](https://downloads.tuxfamily.org/godotengine/4.0/Godot_v4.0-stable_changelog_authors.txt)
- [Git changelog (chronological)](https://downloads.tuxfamily.org/godotengine/4.0/Godot_v4.0-stable_changelog_chrono.txt)
- Download (GitHub): Expand **Assets** below
- [Download (TuxFamily)](https://downloads.tuxfamily.org/godotengine/4.0)
- [Migration guide for Godot 3 users](https://docs.godotengine.org/en/4.0/tutorials/migrating/upgrading_to_godot_4.html)
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the godotengine/godot release 4.0-stable, Godot 4.0 is the biggest release so far, with 4 years of development, 12,000 merged pull requests, 7,000 fixed issues, and 1,500 contributors. All parts of the engine have been modernized, refactored, overhauled, rewritten, and redesigned, marking the start of the new, modern Godot Engine.

chunks.title: 4.1-stable
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: godotengine/godot
Release: 4.1-stable
Tag: 4.1-stable; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2023-07-06T08:37:13Z; created_at: 2023-07-05T14:22:00Z
Release notes:
**Godot 4.1** is a feature release improving upon Godot 4.0 in all aspects, making it more performant, reliable and feature-packed. It inaugurates the 4-month release cycle that we're now using for minor releases, ensuring a short path from development to production use, and making it easy for users to upgrade from one minor to the next and stay up-to-date with the Godot ecosystem.
- [Release notes](https://godotengine.org/article/godot-4-1-is-here)
- [Curated changelog](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/4.1-stable/CHANGELOG.md)
- [Git changelog (by authors)](https://downloads.tuxfamily.org/godotengine/4.1/Godot_v4.1-stable_changelog_authors.txt)
- [Git changelog (chronological)](https://downloads.tuxfamily.org/godotengine/4.1/Godot_v4.1-stable_changelog_chrono.txt)
- Download (GitHub): Expand **Assets** below
- [Download (TuxFamily)](https://downloads.tuxfamily.org/godotengine/4.1)
*All files for this release are mirrored under **Assets** below.*
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the godotengine/godot release 4.1-stable, Godot 4.1 is a feature release that improves upon Godot 4.0 in all aspects, making it more performant, reliable, and feature-packed. It inaugurates the 4-month release cycle for minor releases.

chunks.title: 4.1.3-stable
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: godotengine/godot
Release: 4.1.3-stable
Tag: 4.1.3-stable; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2023-11-01T21:09:46Z; created_at: 2023-11-01T15:35:14Z
Release notes:
**Godot 4.1.3** is a maintenance release addressing stability and usability issues, and fixing all sorts of bugs. Maintenance releases are compatible with previous releases and are recommended for adoption.
Report bugs on GitHub after checking that they haven't been reported:
- https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues
----
- [Release notes](https://godotengine.org/article/maintenance-release-godot-4-1-3/)
- [Complete changelog](https://godotengine.github.io/godot-interactive-changelog/#4.1.3)
- [Curated changelog](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/4.1.3-stable/CHANGELOG.md)
- Download (GitHub): Expand **Assets** below
- [Download (TuxFamily)](https://downloads.tuxfamily.org/godotengine/4.1.3)
*All files for this release are mirrored under **Assets** below.*

**Godot 4.2** is a feature release improving upon the previous version in many aspects, such as usability and performance. Feature releases also contain new features, but preserve compatibility with previous releases.
Report bugs on GitHub after checking that they haven't been reported:
- https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues

- [Migration guide](https://docs.godotengine.org/en/stable/tutorials/migrating/upgrading_to_godot_4.2.html)
- [Breaking changes](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pulls?q=is%3Apr+is%3Amerged+label%3A%22breaks+compat%22+milestone%3A4.2)
----

- [Download (TuxFamily)](https://downloads.tuxfamily.org/godotengine/4.2)
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the godotengine/godot release 4.2-stable, Godot 4.2 is a feature release improving upon the previous version in usability and performance, containing new features while preserving compatibility with previous releases.

chunks.title: 3.6-stable
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: godotengine/godot
Release: 3.6-stable
Tag: 3.6-stable; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2024-09-09T08:22:12Z; created_at: 2024-09-08T20:41:28Z
Release notes:
**Godot 3.6** is a feature release improving upon the previous version in many aspects, such as usability and performance. Feature releases also contain new features, but preserve compatibility with previous releases.
*Note: Godot 3.6 is intended for users who are currently using Godot 3.5, and would benefit from new features and bug fixes in the Godot 3 branch. For new projects, we recommend using the latest Godot 4 stable release, which receives more frequent updates and community support.*
Report bugs on GitHub after checking that they haven't been reported:
- https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues
----
- [Release announcement](https://godotengine.org/article/godot-3-6-finally-released/)
- [Interactive changelog](https://godotengine.github.io/godot-interactive-changelog/#3.6) ([text version](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/3.6-stable/CHANGELOG.md))

chunks.memory_unit_text: In the godotengine/godot release 3.6-stable, Godot 3.6 is a feature release improving upon the previous version in usability and performance, containing new features while preserving compatibility with previous releases. It is intended for users currently using Godot 3.5 who would benefit from new features and bug fixes in the Godot 3 branch.

questions.query_text: In the godotengine/godot releases, which release introduces a new NavigationServer with obstacle avoidance, a Time singleton, and asynchronous shader compilation?

answer_support.answer_text: new NavigationServer with obstacle avoidance; Time singleton; asynchronous shader compilation and caching

questions.query_text: Across the godotengine/godot releases, how does the change described in Godot 4.0-stable differ from the change described in Godot 4.1-stable?

options.text: 4.0-stable is a massive overhaul of the entire engine with 12,000 merged pull requests, while 4.1-stable is a feature release that improves performance and reliability and introduces a 4-month release cycle.
4.0-stable introduces a 4-month release cycle, while 4.1-stable is a maintenance release fixing bugs.
Both releases are maintenance releases that only fix bugs without adding new features.
4.0-stable adds a NavigationServer, while 4.1-stable adds asynchronous shader compilation.

questions.expected_answer: 4.0-stable is a massive overhaul of the entire engine with 12,000 merged pull requests, while 4.1-stable is a feature release that improves performance and reliability and introduces a 4-month release cycle.

answer_support.answer_text: 4 years of development. 12,000 merged pull requests. 7,000 fixed issues. 1,500 individual contributors ... all parts of the engine have been modernized, refactored, overhauled, rewritten, redesigned.
feature release improving upon Godot 4.0 in all aspects, making it more performant, reliable and feature-packed. It inaugurates the 4-month release cycle

questions.query_text: In the godotengine/godot release window, order the following release-content states from earliest to latest: (1) a release that is a major milestone with a new NavigationServer and asynchronous shader compilation, (2) a release that is the biggest release so far with 12,000 merged pull requests and a complete engine overhaul, (3) a feature release that inaugurates the 4-month release cycle, (4) a maintenance release that fixes an iOS export template issue for Xcode < 15.

answer_support.answer_text: Godot 3.5 is a major new milestone ... new NavigationServer with obstacle avoidance ... asynchronous shader compilation and caching
The Godot 4.0 release is by all metrics our biggest release so far. ... 12,000 merged pull requests ... all parts of the engine have been modernized, refactored, overhauled, rewritten, redesigned.
Godot 4.1 is a feature release ... inaugurates the 4-month release cycle
The file Godot_v4.1.2-stable_export_templates.tpz was updated on October 11, 2023 to fix an issue with the iOS export template, preventing from exporting to iOS with Xcode < 15.
